name: Side Street Inn
tagline: Where Honolulu chefs eat on their day off — the fried rice feeds four and costs sixteen bucks.
mustOrder: pan-fried pork chops ($16–20, THE signature dish)
talkStory: Colin Nishida opened Side Street Inn in 1992 as a neighborhood bar, then his pan-fried pork chops got so famous he earned a James Beard semifinalist nod. After Honolulu's top chefs close their own kitchens at 10-11pm, they come here — this is literally where the restaurant industry eats. Anthony Bourdain visited and called it essential. Order family-style, 3-4 dishes for two people. The fried rice portion feeds a small army for $16.
